content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Self-Debiasing Prompts for Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models often propagate social biases and stereotypes in their outputs. Current prompting strategies only partially mitigate these biases, leaving room for more effective interventions.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed in sensitive applications, reducing harmful bias is critical for fairness and trust. Prompting-based solutions are model-agnostic, low-cost, and updatable post-deployment. New strategies are needed as LLMs become more pervasive in society.",
  "method": "We propose 'counterfactual self-debiasing prompts' that interleave a target query with a counterfactual version generated via prompt-based attribute swapping (e.g., gender, ethnicity). The model is instructed to answer both versions, then compare and explicitly justify any differences in its responses, discouraging reliance on stereotypes. This prompt encourages the LLM to self-reflect and minimize unjustified bias-induced changes. No tuning or external classifiers are used; the method is fully prompt-based.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on the CrowS-Pairs and StereoSet datasets, measuring stereotypical bias using metrics like SEAT (Sentence Encoder Association Test) and language modeling accuracy. Baselines include standard prompting, anti-bias chain-of-thought, and bias-aware few-shot prompting. Effectiveness will be measured by reduction in bias scores without performance degradation."
}
